cards.back: Insertion Sort is a simple sorting algorithm that works by dividing the input into a sorted and an unsorted region. Each subsequent element from the unsorted region is inserted into the sorted region at its correct position.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort?
cards.back: The time complexity of Bubble Sort is O(n^2) in the worst case and O(n) in the best case.
Selection Sort works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted region and swapping it with the first element of the unsorted region.

cards.front: What is the primary difference between Bubble Sort and Insertion Sort?
cards.back: The primary difference between Bubble Sort and Insertion Sort is that Bubble Sort is a comparison-based algorithm that repeatedly swapped adjacent elements, while Insertion Sort is also a comparison-based algorithm but it inserts elements in the sorted region one by one.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort?
cards.back: The time complexity of Selection Sort is O(n^2) in all cases.
